description: Generative asset provider registry — the single source of truth for which AI generation provider and model serves each asset capability. This whole layer is OPTIONAL: it activates only when a provider's apiKeyEnv is set in the environment; without a key the scaffold uses its built-in placeholder/procedural asset tooling as the default (see rules/common/asset-pipeline.md). scripts/generate-assets.js resolves capability-to-model routing from this file; swapping a default model or adding a provider is a manifest edit, not a code edit. Model catalogs rotate quickly: verify a model id against the provider catalog before relying on it in a new project.

notes: Chosen as default because one pay-per-use API key covers every asset modality (3D, image, audio, video) with no monthly subscription.
All endpoints use the async queue API: POST https://queue.fal.run/<model> returns request_id + status_url + response_url.
Review fal.ai and upstream model license terms before shipping generated content commercially.
An optional MCP server ('fal-media' in mcp-configs/mcp-servers.json) exposes the same catalog interactively; the script remains the reproducible path.

image.notes: Sprites, textures, UI art, concept art. Iterate on nano-banana-2 (fast/cheap), switch to nano-banana-pro for finals. For tileable textures, ask for 'seamless tileable texture' explicitly and verify edges.

skybox.notes: Prompt for a '360 degree equirectangular panorama' and validate the horizon line before engine import; engines expect a 2:1 equirectangular projection for sky materials.

model3d.notes: Tencent Hunyuan3D. GLB output imports into Unity, Unreal, Godot, and Three.js without conversion. Use the image-to-3d variant to lift accepted concept art into 3D. Static meshes only — rigging/animation needs a dedicated provider.

sfx.notes: Short gameplay sound effects from a text description. Keep descriptions physical and concrete ('metal coin dropping on stone, short, bright').

music.notes: Background music and stingers. Specify genre, tempo, mood, and loopability in the prompt; verify loop points manually before integration.

speech.notes: Voice-over and character barks. Localized VO must follow the localization pipeline, not ad hoc generation.

video.notes: Intro videos, cinematics, trailers. Image-to-video (the first alternative; pass image_url via --input-json) gives more art-directed control than pure text-to-video. Seedance 2.0 and Veo 3 generate audio natively. Video is the most expensive capability — always dry-run and estimate before batch runs.
